title: Geblang package manifest
description: Schema for geblang.yaml, the package manifest read by the geblang toolchain (geblang, geblang test, geblang build, geblang check). Found by walking up from the current file; geblang.yml and geblang.json are accepted as alternate filenames. See docs/user/07-modules-packages.md and docs/user/13-bundling.md.

name.description: The package namespace and canonical module prefix. With source: src, the file src/app/users.gb is imported as 'import app.users;'. Pick a top-level namespace you own (e.g. acme.*) to avoid colliding with a reserved built-in name.

version.description: Free-form version string. Resolution ignores it; geblang build embeds it as the binary's version (shown by the built binary's --version).

source.description: The primary module root, a single directory. Its tree resolves as modules: 'import app.users;' finds <source>/app/users.gb or <source>/app/users/init.gb. When omitted, the manifest's own directory is the root.

paths.description: Additional module roots, each relative to the manifest, searched after source. Use for generated or co-located code that should also resolve as modules.

modulePaths.description: Alias for paths; accepted and merged in.

resources.description: Files and directories embedded into the binary by 'geblang build', so a single-file release can read them at runtime. Not part of the import tree. Each entry is a directory (embedded recursively) or a glob pattern.

dependencies.description: Other packages this one imports, each mounted under a name and pointing at a local path or a git repository.

additionalProperties.description: A bare string is shorthand for a path dependency. The mapping form supports path or git dependencies.

oneOf.description: Shorthand for a path dependency, e.g. 'shared: ../shared'.

path.description: Path to another local package. A relative path is resolved against the manifest; an absolute path is used as-is. A leading ~ expands to the home directory and $VAR / ${VAR} expand from the environment. The target must itself be a package (have its own geblang.yaml).

git.description: Remote git repository URL. A scheme-less value such as github.com/acme/httplib is treated as https://github.com/acme/httplib. Explicit schemes (https://, ssh://) and the scp-like git@github.com:acme/httplib.git form are used unchanged.

version.description: Tag or branch for a git dependency; 'latest' for the newest semver tag; omit for the default branch.

package.description: A nested block with name/version keys, accepted as an alternative to the top-level name and version fields.

name.description: Alternative to the top-level name field.

version.description: Alternative to the top-level version field.

permissions.description: Declares privileged capabilities (FFI, local ONNX inference, process control, headless-browser automation) baked into a built binary and also enabled for 'geblang run' / 'geblang test' in the project. Off by default.

libraries.description: Allow-list of native shared libraries FFI may load.

path.description: Exact path to an allowed native library.

glob.description: Glob pattern matching allowed native libraries, e.g. /opt/torch/lib/*.so.

extensions.description: Declares polyglot/sandboxed extension processes usable via ext.load / ext.connect, keyed by extension name.

additionalProperties.description: Configuration for a single named extension.

command.description: Command (argv) used to launch the extension process, e.g. ["python3", "extensions/python/extension.py"].

socket.description: Unix socket path for connecting to a pre-started extension.

host.description: Host/address for connecting to a pre-started extension.

startup_timeout_ms.description: Milliseconds to wait for the extension process to become ready before failing.

env.description: Extra environment variables passed to the extension process.
